title: 동료 묘사 4스텝 전략 (Description 질문)
summary: OPIC 체점 기준 4가지: Function(핵심), Text Type(다양성), Content & Context(집중), Accuracy — 4스텝 전략이 이 순서를 자연스럽게 커버한다
Step 1: 핵심 메인 포인트를 한 문장으로 명확하게 — '나는 ~를 존경한다, 왜냐면 항상 긍정적이기 때문'
Step 2: 다양성 — 핵심 외 다른 좋은 특성 2가지를 예시로 보여줘 텍스트 타입 점수 확보
Step 3: Step 1 핵심으로 돌아오며 더 깊이 — 반전(and yet / but)으로 구체적 상황 추가
Step 4: 깔끔한 마무리 — 'in a nutshell'처럼 요약 표현으로 끝내기
한국인 자주 실수: 'she looks hard(X)' → 'going through a hard time(O)', 'my condition is bad(X)' → 'I'm feeling pretty bad(O)'
'I want to talk about' 대신 'Let me tell you about'가 훨씬 자연스럽고, one of my seniors처럼 집단 속 한 명임을 표현하면 더 정확

keyExpressions.en: Let me tell you about one of my seniors at work
keyExpressions.ko: 직장 선배 중 한 명에 대해 얘기해 볼게요
keyExpressions.note: 'I want to talk about'보다 훨씬 자연스러운 시작

keyExpressions.en: I really admire her because she's always so positive
keyExpressions.note: admire = respect와 유사, so로 감정 강조

keyExpressions.en: Of course, she has other great qualities as well
keyExpressions.note: Step 2 시작 전환 표현, of course가 자연스러운 연결

keyExpressions.en: For example, she's never late for work
She also buys coffee for the team almost every day, which I think is awesome
keyExpressions.ko: 거의 매일 팀한테 커피를 사준다, 그게 정말 좋다고 생각한다

keyExpressions.en: The one thing I love about her the most is that she's never negative
keyExpressions.ko: 내가 그녀에 대해 제일 좋아하는 한 가지는 절대 부정적이지 않다는 것이다
keyExpressions.note: Step 3 핵심 복귀 패턴, 강력한 문장 구조

keyExpressions.en: She's actually pregnant now, and yet she never complains
keyExpressions.ko: 사실 지금 임신 중인데, 그럼에도 불구하고 절대 불평하지 않는다
keyExpressions.note: and yet = 역접 접속사, 훨씬 부드럽고 세련된 표현

keyExpressions.en: She's always hard-working, which I truly respect
keyExpressions.ko: 항상 열심히 일한다, 그게 정말 존경스럽다
keyExpressions.note: which I truly respect — 의견/감정을 which로 붙이는 패턴

keyExpressions.en: So yeah, that's my senior in a nutshell
keyExpressions.ko: 그러니까, 한마디로 내 선배는 그런 사람이다
keyExpressions.note: Step 4 마무리, in a nutshell = 요약하자면

keyExpressions.en: She is going through a hard time
keyExpressions.note: 'she looks hard(X)' 대신 쓸 자연스러운 표현
'My condition is bad(X)' 대신 — condition은 사물에 쓰는 게 자연스러움

cards.example: Let me tell you about one of my seniors at work.

cards.front: 직장 선배 중 한 명 (집단 속 한 명 표현)
cards.back: one of my seniors at work
cards.example: Let me tell you about one of my seniors at work.
I really admire her because she's always so positive.
I really admire her because she's always so positive.

cards.front: 물론 다른 좋은 점들도 있다 (Step 2 시작)
cards.back: Of course, she has other great qualities as well.
cards.example: Of course, she has other great qualities as well.
She also buys coffee for the team almost every day, which I think is awesome.

cards.front: 내가 ~에 대해 제일 좋아하는 한 가지는 ~이다 (Step 3 핵심 복귀)
cards.back: The one thing I love about [her] the most is that...
cards.example: The one thing I love about her the most is that she's never negative.

cards.front: 사실 지금 임신 중인데, 그럼에도 불구하고 절대 불평하지 않는다
cards.back: She's actually pregnant now, and yet she never complains.
cards.example: She's actually pregnant now, and yet she never complains.

cards.front: 항상 열심히 일한다 — 그게 정말 존경스럽다 (which 패턴)
cards.back: She's always hard-working, which I truly respect.
cards.example: She's always hard-working, which I truly respect.
So yeah, that's my senior in a nutshell.

cards.front: 힘든 시간을 보내고 있다 (she looks hard 대신)
cards.back: She is going through a hard time.
cards.example: She is going through a hard time, but she never gives up.

cards.front: 컨디션이 안 좋다 (my condition is bad 대신)
cards.example: I'm feeling pretty bad today, but I'll manage.
